---
title: "Networking"
subtitle: "Computer Networking Fundamentals"
toc: true
toc-location: right
toc-depth: 3
number-sections: true
smooth-scroll: true
---

# Introduction

This script covers the fundamentals of computer networking. Topics include the OSI model, protocols, IP addressing, and routing.

# The OSI Model

The Open Systems Interconnection (OSI) model structures network communication into seven layers.

| Layer | Name | Function | Example Protocols |
|-------|------|----------|-------------------|
| 7 | Application | User-facing services | HTTP, FTP, DNS |
| 6 | Presentation | Data formatting & encryption | SSL/TLS, JPEG |
| 5 | Session | Session management | NetBIOS, RPC |
| 4 | Transport | Reliable delivery | TCP, UDP |
| 3 | Network | Logical addressing & routing | IP, ICMP |
| 2 | Data Link | Physical addressing | Ethernet, Wi-Fi |
| 1 | Physical | Bit transmission | Cables, Signals |

## Encapsulation

Data is wrapped with headers as it moves down the stack:

$$
\text{Data} \xrightarrow{L7-L5} \text{Segment} \xrightarrow{L4} \text{Packet} \xrightarrow{L3} \text{Frame} \xrightarrow{L2} \text{Bits}
$$

# IP Addressing

## IPv4

An IPv4 address is a 32-bit number, typically written in dotted-decimal notation:

$$
\underbrace{192.168.1.0}_{\text{Network}} \;/\; \underbrace{24}_{\text{Prefix Length}}
$$

```{python}
import ipaddress

network = ipaddress.ip_network("192.168.1.0/24")
print(f"Network: {network.network_address}")
print(f"Broadcast: {network.broadcast_address}")
print(f"Hosts: {network.num_addresses - 2}")
```

## Subnetting

Subnetting divides a network into smaller sub-networks.

```{python}
network = ipaddress.ip_network("10.0.0.0/16")
subnets = list(network.subnets(prefixlen_diff=4))
for subnet in subnets[:4]:
    print(f"Subnet: {subnet}, Hosts: {subnet.num_addresses - 2}")
print(f"... ({len(subnets)} subnets total)")
```

# Transport Layer

## TCP vs UDP

| Feature | TCP | UDP |
|---------|-----|-----|
| Connection | Connection-oriented | Connectionless |
| Reliability | Guaranteed delivery | Best-effort |
| Ordering | Ordered | Unordered |
| Overhead | Higher | Lower |
| Use case | Web, Email | Streaming, DNS |

# Routing

*Section to be expanded: static routing, dynamic routing, routing protocols (OSPF, BGP).*

# Application Layer Protocols

*Section to be expanded: HTTP, DNS, DHCP.*
